meta.setting: Roads, switchbacks, creek approaches, and mining routes around French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with open-country travel, route choice, camp friction, and reputation pressure

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Gold rush road movement, Clear Creek communities, freight, settlement, and regional travel pressure.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Northern California mining commerce, road routes, stage movement, and county-town material.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Road Scouts cast page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON is only shape and guidance.
page_shape: square image reference immediately after the title
opening blockquote with invented road warning, scout note, or grade saying generated later
H2 archetype subtitle in the register of Ahead-Riders
one or two compact lore paragraphs introducing them as advance riders who test roads, watch switchbacks, and read danger before the wagon arrives
H3 Role with a short table-facing function phrase
H3 Traits with two to four terse signs or habits
10 H4 headings, each followed later by a brief playable move, leverage, cost, edge, or complication
H3 Camp Say closing blockquote with plain meaning generated later
generation_guidance: Make road scouts practical and underpaid: they know shortcuts, ambush bends, porch watchers, washouts, bad bridges, and which hill will break a tired team.
Tie future moves to route choice, threat spotting, false trail, supply warnings, local passage, dust reading, and deciding whether to push after dark.
Give them old road language built from grade, hoofprint, mud color, smoke, bird movement, and the way a town goes quiet before strangers arrive.
Let the page support both player scouts and NPC scouts hired by stage lines, claim bosses, families, or worried tribal households.
Do not include final move names or final move text in this JSON.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: They should feel like the first person awake and the last person thanked. The scout does not find the road; the scout decides what the road will cost.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A road-scout scene should make the table choose between the shorter way, the safer way, the legal way, and the way nobody wants named.
must_preserve: Use 1899 western road and travel language only.
Keep route knowledge physical and social, not supernatural.
The generated page must include image path images/road-scouts.jpg.
No finished page prose, final moves, or final sayings belong in this prompt file.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not include medieval, enchanted, sacred, or fake-tribal framing
do not make scouting omniscient; fatigue, weather, local prejudice, and bad information should matter
do not use modern slang, modern gear, vehicles, or clean theme-park staging
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about travel judgment, route pressure, and table action.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
